title: Silky Jain Apples Polish
description: A delicious and silky polish dish made with apples and a hint of cinnamon, perfect for a relaxing brunch. This recipe is a variation of a traditional polish dish adapted to be jain-friendly.
instructions: Peel, core, and slice the apples into thin pieces.
In a large saucepan, heat the coconut oil over medium heat.
Add the sliced apples, cinnamon, ginger, cardamom powder, and salt. Cook for 5 minutes.
Add the honey and lemon juice to the apples and stir well.
Pour in the water and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and let the apples simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until they are tender and the liquid has reduced.
Stir in the turmeric and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Serve the silky apples polish warm, garnished with a sprinkle of cinnamon or cardamom powder.
Let the dish cool completely before refrigerating or freezing for later use.
Reheat the apples polish by simmering it over low heat, adding a little water if needed.
Serve the silky apples polish with your favorite breakfast items, such as toast, yogurt, or oatmeal.
Experiment with different spices and flavor combinations to create your own unique version of this dish.
